blocks.content: It wasn't a question. He looked at her.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What makes you think I'm nervous?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You've been pulling at the grass for ten minutes.
He looked at his hand. He had pulled up a small pile of grass without knowing it. He let it go.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I just do. Can you feel it?
She was quiet for long enough that he thought she was going to say no, that he was being dramatic, which would have been the right thing to say and also very much a Daisy move.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Then why do you seem fine?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Because being fine and being bothered aren't the same thing.

The movie kept going. The park kept making its park sounds. Somewhere behind them a little kid laughed at something and their parent shushed them gently, and Kit thought that maybe that was it — that you could hold two things at once and the trick was just not to drop either one.
